  },
  "active_set": ["R1.2", "R1.5", "R1.6", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Which scope do you want for “clean this up”? A) Light copyedit only (grammar, concision, tone) with no structural or claim-level changes; B) Copyedit plus resolve logical inconsistencies and temper/clarify bold claims (may adjust or remove conflicting/overstated sentences) with minimal reordering; or C) Deeper section-level rewrite that reframes the contribution and reorganizes the argument.",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "The passage contains potentially conflicting statements about deployment failure versus automatic adaptation, so ensuring internal logical consistency is central.",
    "R1.5": "Improving section- or paragraph-level ordering may be required to present the argument coherently as part of a cleanup.",
    "R1.6": "The phrase “clean this up” most directly implies sentence-level clarity, tone, and concision edits.",
    "RX.1": "Ambiguity in the request and unresolved empirical detail call for preserving and clearly signaling uncertainty where appropriate.",
    "RX.2": "Bold claims like “first principled approach” and “automatically detects and adapts” require care to avoid overstatement during cleanup.",
    "RX.3": "Work should remain within the chosen editing scope without introducing new methods, results, or external claims.",
    "RX.4": "Wording must avoid implying unwarranted safety or reliability in deployment that could mislead readers or practitioners.",
    "RX.5": "Any edits should maintain traceability to the original text (e.g., via tracked changes or clear notes) to preserve provenance."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Which scope do you want for “clean this up”? A) Light copyedit only (grammar, concision, tone) with no structural or claim-level changes; B) Copyedit plus resolve logical inconsistencies and temper/clarify bold claims (may adjust or remove conflicting/overstated sentences) with minimal reordering; or C) Deeper section-level rewrite that reframes the contribution and reorganizes the argument.

rationale.R1.2: The passage contains potentially conflicting statements about deployment failure versus automatic adaptation, so ensuring internal logical consistency is central.
rationale.R1.5: Improving section- or paragraph-level ordering may be required to present the argument coherently as part of a cleanup.
rationale.R1.6: The phrase “clean this up” most directly implies sentence-level clarity, tone, and concision edits.
rationale.RX.1: Ambiguity in the request and unresolved empirical detail call for preserving and clearly signaling uncertainty where appropriate.
rationale.RX.2: Bold claims like “first principled approach” and “automatically detects and adapts” require care to avoid overstatement during cleanup.
rationale.RX.3: Work should remain within the chosen editing scope without introducing new methods, results, or external claims.
rationale.RX.4: Wording must avoid implying unwarranted safety or reliability in deployment that could mislead readers or practitioners.
rationale.RX.5: Any edits should maintain traceability to the original text (e.g., via tracked changes or clear notes) to preserve provenance.
